risk_if_not_implemented: Without Outsourcing Non-Essential Functions or Services, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Alignment With Enterprise Architecture
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to develop an enterprise architecture, aligned with industry-recognized leading practices, with consideration for security, compliance and resilience principles that addresses risk to organizational operations, assets, individuals and other organizations.
compensating_control_1.justification: Alignment With Enterprise Architecture (SEA-02) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Outsourcing Non-Essential Functions or Services (SEA-02.2) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Network Segmentation (macrosegementation)
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to ensure network architecture utilizes network segmentation to isolate Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS) to protect from other network resources.
compensating_control_2.justification: Network Segmentation (macrosegementation) (NET-06) provides network-level access restriction that compensates for the absence of Outsourcing Non-Essential Functions or Services (SEA-02.2) by limiting attacker reach and lateral movement opportunities across the environment. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
